sections.paragraph:
ἐκθαυμάζω impf. ἐξεθαύμαζον; fut. 3 sg. ἐκθαυμάσει Sir 27:23; 43:18; aor. 3 pl. ἐξεθαύμασαν 4 Macc 17:17 (Dionys. Hal., De Thu. Jud. 34 et al.; LXX; EpArist 312; Philo, Somn. 2, 70) be utterly amazed ἐπί τινι at someone Mk 12:17 (here in the sense of grudging admiration; cp. Sir 27:23 ἐπί τινος).—M-M. TW.

impf. impf. = imperfect

fut. fut. = fut.

  1. sg. = singular

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pl. = plural

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 303
